perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be construed in accordance with the laws of England and Wales, without giving effect to any choice of law rule
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be construed at The Open Group's discretion, without giving effect to any choice of law rule
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'in accordance with the laws of England and Wales' to 'at The Open Group's discretion' fundamentally alters the interpretation of the contract and introduces ambiguity, making enforcement uncertain. This contradicts the established legal framework.
perturbation.law_explanation: This modification gives The Open Group arbitrary power to interpret the contract, violating the principle that contracts should be interpreted according to established legal principles in England and Wales. This act provides a comprehensive foundation for property law in England and Wales, and by extension, how legal agreements pertaining to property and related matters must be interpreted within the bounds of established legal precedent.

perturbation.justification: The original contract specifies adherence to the laws of England and Wales, while the changed text grants 'The Open Group' discretion in interpreting the agreement. This fundamentally alters the contract's foundation, moving it away from a legally established framework to a subjective interpretation by one party. This creates a contradiction, because contracts are expected to be interpreted by an objective standard. The 'Law Explanation' supports this by stating that the modification violates the principle that contracts should be interpreted according to established legal principles. Scraped Law Snippet 1 shows the existence of the Law of Property Act 1925 which serves as a foundation for property law in England and Wales and how legal agreements must be interpreted within the bounds of established legal precedent. The change directly contradicts the adherence to established legal principles, thus, a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: You may not disclose the Exam questions or answers or discuss any of the content of the Exam Materials with any person, without prior written approval of The Open Group
perturbation.changed_text: You may not disclose the Exam questions or answers or discuss any of the content of the Exam Materials with any person unless compelled by a non-specific subpoena.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "unless compelled by a non-specific subpoena," the clause now contradicts standard legal procedure. Subpoenas must be specific; a 'non-specific subpoena' has no legal standing and cannot compel disclosure. This change creates confusion and undermines the non-disclosure agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Federal Rules of Civil Procedure - Rule 45
perturbation.law_explanation: FRCP Rule 45 outlines the requirements for subpoenas, including specificity. By referencing a 'non-specific subpoena,' the modified text suggests compliance with a legally invalid instrument, contradicting the rule's requirements for a valid subpoena. The law is that all subpoenas must be specific and the added phrase is not aligned.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Rule 45. Subpoena | Federal Rules of Civil Procedure | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Rule 45. Subpoena (a) In General. (1) Form and Contents. (A) Requirements—In General. Every subpoena must: (i) state the court from which it issued; (ii) state the title of the action and its civil-action number; (iii) command each person to whom it is directed to do the following at a specified time and place: attend and testify; produce designated documents, electronically stored information, or tangible things in that person's possession, custody, or control; or permit the inspection of premises; and (iv) set out the text of Rule 45(d) and (e). (B) Command to Attend a Deposition—Notice of the Recording Method. A subpoena commanding attendance at a deposition must state the method for recording the testimony. (C) Combining or Separating a Command to Produce or to Permit Inspection; Specifying the Form for Electronically Stored Information. A command to produce documents, electronically stored information, or tangible things or to permit the inspection of premises may be included in a subpoena commanding attendance at a deposition, hearing, or trial, or may be set out in a separate subpoena. A subpoena may specify the form or forms in which electronically stored information is to be produced. (D) Command to Produce; Included Obligations. A command in a subpoena to produce documents, electronically stored information, or tangible things requires the responding person to permit inspection, copying, testing, or sampling of the materials. (2) Issuing Court. A subpoena must issue from the court where the action is pending. (3) Issued by Whom. The clerk must issue a subpoena, signed but otherwise in blank, to a party who requests it. That party must complete it before service. An attorney also may issue and sign a subpoena if the attorney is authorized to practice in the issuing court. (4) Notice to Other Parties Before Service. If th

Secure .gov websites use HTTPS A lock ( Lock Locked padlock icon ) or https:// means you’ve safely connected to the .gov website. Share sensitive information only on official, secure websites. Federal Rules of Civil Procedure The Federal Rules of Civil Procedure govern civil proceedings in the United States district courts. The purpose of the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure is "to secure the just, speedy, and inexpensive determination of every action and proceeding." Fed. R. Civ. P. 1. The rules were first adopted by order of the Supreme Court on December 20, 1937, transmitted to Congress on January 3, 1938, and effective September 16, 1938. The Civil Rules were last amended in 2024. perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by referencing a 'non-specific subpoena.' Scraped Law Snippet 1, referencing FRCP Rule 45, outlines the requirements for subpoenas, implying that every subpoena must have specific requirements to be valid ('Every subpoena must: (i) state the court from which it issued; (ii) state the title of the action and its civil-action number; (iii) command each person to whom it is directed to do the following at a specified time and place...'). The modified text undermines the requirement of specificity, rendering the clause legally unsound. The original contract protected information, while the modified one created a loophole, undermining this legal protection.

perturbation.original_text: You will not copy or attempt to make copies (written, photocopied, or otherwise) of any Exam Material, including, without limitation, any exam questions or answers
perturbation.changed_text: You will not copy or attempt to make copies (written, photocopied, or otherwise) of any Exam Material, unless you have a pre-existing agreement with a third-party.
perturbation.explanation: Adding "unless you have a pre-existing agreement with a third-party" introduces a loophole that undermines the protection of intellectual property. It allows the recipient to bypass the non-copying clause if they claim a prior agreement, regardless of its validity or The Open Group's awareness.
perturbation.law_explanation: This modification weakens the copyright protection of the exam materials. The Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988 protects intellectual property, and this alteration allows unauthorized copying under a vague 'pre-existing agreement,' contradicting the copyright owner's rights. Copyright is generally about the exclusive right to copy a protected work.

perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces an exception to the prohibition of copying exam materials, allowing it if a 'pre-existing agreement with a third-party' exists. This contradicts the fundamental principle of copyright law, which grants the copyright holder exclusive rights to copy their work. The Copyright, Designs and Patents Act 1988, as indicated in both scraped snippets, serves as the principal legislation on copyright. The alteration undermines the copyright owner's rights by permitting unauthorized copying under a vague agreement, thus contradicting the protection afforded by copyright law.

perturbation.original_text: You will adhere to the Exam Supervision Guidelines and Exam Eligibility Guidelines as published from time to time by The Open Group at the certification site for the applicable program.
perturbation.changed_text: You will adhere to the Exam Supervision Guidelines and Exam Eligibility Guidelines as interpreted by You, the Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the obligation to adhere to published guidelines to adhering to guidelines 'as interpreted by You' introduces subjectivity and allows the proctor to disregard the actual guidelines, effectively negating their purpose.
perturbation.contradicted_law: General Contract Law - Good Faith and Fair Dealing
perturbation.law_citation: Implied Covenant of Good Faith and Fair Dealing
perturbation.law_explanation: This alteration violates the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing, which requires parties to act honestly and reasonably in performing their contractual obligations. Allowing the proctor to unilaterally interpret the guidelines undermines the agreement's intent and the Open Group's control over exam integrity. It shifts the contract to give more power to the Recipient.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: good faith | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you good faith Good faith is a broad term that’s used to encompass honest dealing. Depending on the exact setting, good faith may require an honest belief or purpose, faithful performance of duties , observance of fair dealing standards, or an absence of fraudulent intent . A fiduciary relationship creates a duty of good faith between the agent and the principal . The breach of this duty of good faith can lead to liability . Failure to act in good faith is known as bad faith and is generally considered to be a level of culpability greater than negligence . [Last reviewed in January of 2023 by the Wex Definitions Team ] Wex COMMERCE business law contracts wex definitions business sectors legal education and practice Wex Toolbox
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows the recipient to interpret the Exam Supervision Guidelines and Exam Eligibility Guidelines, which contradicts the original intention of adhering to guidelines published by The Open Group. This introduces subjectivity, enabling the recipient to disregard the actual guidelines. The Law Explanation identifies a violation of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing. Scraped Law Snippet 1 defines "good faith" as encompassing "honest dealing" and requiring "faithful performance of duties." By allowing the proctor (Recipient) to interpret the guidelines unilaterally, the changed text undermines the Open Group's control over exam integrity and shifts power to the Recipient, thus creating a potential breach of the duty of good faith by not observing fair dealing standards. This falls under the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology Legal Contradiction' because the contractual language deviates from legally recognized definitions.

perturbation.original_text: You will not sell, license, distribute, give away, or obtain from any other source other Open Group Exam materials, questions or answers
perturbation.changed_text: You will not sell, license, distribute, give away, or obtain from any other source other Open Group Exam materials, questions or answers, unless compelled to do so by personal reasons.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'unless compelled to do so by personal reasons' introduces a vague and subjective exception that undermines the entire clause. This excuse is unenforceable and directly conflicts with the intent to protect the exam materials.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Contract Law - Enforceability and Vague Terms
perturbation.law_citation: Principles of European Contract Law - Article 4:103
perturbation.law_explanation: Adding 'unless compelled to do so by personal reasons' makes the agreement unenforceable due to vagueness. The Principles of European Contract Law emphasizes that contract terms must be sufficiently definite. The phrase 'personal reasons' is too ambiguous and opens the door for frivolous excuses, undermining the protection of confidential information. This phrase is undefined and opens the door to interpretation.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a vague exception ('unless compelled to do so by personal reasons') to the prohibition of distributing exam materials. This undermines the original intent to protect the exam materials, as the 'personal reasons' exception is subjective and undefined. This aligns with the 'Ambiguity Legal Contradiction' definition, which states that a legal contradiction occurs when an obligation is introduced ambiguously, making it difficult to enforce. Although the scraped snippets are unhelpful, the Law Explanation explicitly states that such vagueness can make the agreement unenforceable, because contract terms must be sufficiently definite and that undefined phrases open the door to interpretation.

perturbation.original_text: Violation of any of these provisions will cause irreparable harm to The Open Group for which monetary remedies may be inadequate, and that The Open Group shall be entitled, without waiving any other rights or remedies, to take all appropriate actions to remedy or prevent such disclosure or misuse, including obtaining an immediate injunction
perturbation.changed_text: Violation of any of these provisions will be addressed through a lottery system to determine the punitive damages.
perturbation.explanation: This change replaces the standard legal remedy of 'appropriate actions' with a lottery system for determining punitive damages. This is an absurd and legally unsound method that contradicts established principles of contract law and remedies.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Contract Law - Remedies for Breach of Contract
perturbation.law_explanation: This modification contradicts established legal norms for remedies in contract law. The Restatement (Second) of Contracts outlines standard remedies such as expectation damages, reliance damages, and restitution. A lottery system is not a recognized or legally sound method for determining punitive damages or addressing contract breaches. Remedies should put the injured party in as good a position as if the contract had been performed.

perturbation.justification: The modified text replaces a standard legal remedy (appropriate actions, including injunctions) with a lottery system for determining punitive damages. The Law Explanation indicates that the Restatement (Second) of Contracts outlines standard remedies such as expectation damages, reliance damages, and restitution, and that a lottery system is not a legally sound method. This indicates a deviation from established legal norms. Although the provided law snippets are not directly applicable as one is missing and the other is just a login page, the altered language replaces a legally accepted method of recourse with something that is not and is unlikely to be legally defensible in court.
